🌊 Fact Finder Lab • Island Field Report

Report Text

Factual Reports: Reporting Facts Clearly

Observe. Classify. Describe facts. Bab ini membantu siswa memahami fakta umum tentang benda, hewan, tumbuhan, tempat, pekerjaan, teknologi, dan fenomena di sekitar kehidupan kepulauan.

Mulai Membaca Model TextCoba QuizWriting Mission
2struktur utama: general classification + description
8model factual reports lengkap
50+kosakata bilingual dan contoh lokal
6skills: listening, speaking, reading, viewing, writing, presenting

1 • Opening / Hero Text

From Observation to Factual Writing

Beginner Friendly

Bayangkan kamu berjalan di sekitar Pulau Sapuka. Kamu melihat rumput laut yang dijemur, perahu bersandar, nelayan menyiapkan jaring, pantai terkena ombak, sekolah di pulau kecil, atau internet satelit yang membantu siswa belajar. Semua itu bisa dijelaskan sebagai fakta.

Descriptive Text

Menjelaskan satu benda/tempat/orang tertentu.

Sapuka Island is a small island in Pangkep.

Report Text

Menjelaskan fakta umum tentang kelompok/jenis/kategori.

Islands are land areas surrounded by water.

Big Idea

Report Text is used to describe general facts about a class or group of things. Dalam Bahasa Indonesia, Report Text dapat dipahami sebagai laporan faktual umum.

Pertanyaan Pemantik

  • Apa fakta umum tentang rumput laut?
  • Apa ciri-ciri terumbu karang?
  • Apa fungsi kapal bagi masyarakat kepulauan?

Report Text Menjawab

  • What is it?
  • Where does it live or exist?
  • What does it have?
  • What can it do?

Topik Lokal

Seaweed, boats, fishermen, coral reefs, small islands, schools, clean water, and internet access.

Visual Assets

Gambar-gambar Terpisah yang Sudah Dimasukkan

9 posters embedded

Setiap gambar dipasang pada bagian yang sesuai: reading text, viewing zone, vocabulary, dan contoh report visual.

Seaweed

Fact file tentang marine plant.

Boats

Water transportation and parts.

Coral Reefs

Underwater ecosystems.

Small Islands

Map-style island environment.

Fishermen

Coastal job report.

Starlink Internet

Satellite internet report.

Schools

Remote island education.

Clean Water

Water resources on islands.

Classification

Living vs non-living things.

2–3 • Learning Map & Objectives

Learning Journey

1

Observe

Mengamati objek/topik di sekitar: seaweed, boats, fishermen, clean water, school, and island life.

2

Classify

Membedakan topik specific dan general, lalu menentukan kategori.

3

Collect Facts

Mencatat ciri, tempat, fungsi, manfaat, dan tantangan.

4

Build Sentences

Menggunakan Simple Present, is/are, has/have, can, and technical vocabulary.

5

Write

Menyusun general classification dan description menjadi Report Text.

6

Present

Mempresentasikan factual report dengan poster, fact card, atau infographic.

Learning Objectives

Listening

Memahami ide utama dan informasi spesifik dalam factual report lisan.

Speaking

Menyampaikan fakta umum menggunakan pola sederhana.

Reading

Mengidentifikasi tujuan sosial, struktur, dan language features.

Viewing

Membaca fact file, poster, diagram, dan infografis.

Writing

Menulis Report Text pendek dengan general nouns dan Simple Present.

Presenting

Mempresentasikan laporan faktual dengan visual support.

4–6 • Concept Core

Social Function, Specific vs General, and Text Structure

Social Function

Report Text is used:

  • to give factual information,
  • to describe a class or group of things,
  • to explain general characteristics,
  • to help readers learn facts about the world.

Student-Friendly Meaning

Report Text is like a fact file in paragraph form. It tells us what something is, what it has, where it lives/exist, what it can do, and why it is important.

Compare Text Types

Text TypePurposeFocusExample TitleKey Language
DescriptiveDescribe one specific person/place/thingSpecific objectMy School, Sapuka Islandadjectives, specific details
ReportGive factual information about a group/classGeneral factsSchools, Small Islands, SeaweedSimple Present, general nouns
ExplanationExplain how/why something happensProcess/cause-effectHow Rain Formssequence, cause-effect
ProcedureExplain how to do somethingSteps/instructionsHow to Make Teaimperative verbs
RecountRetell past experiencePast eventsMy Trip to Makassarpast tense

Specific Topic

Topiknya khusus, satu, dan jelas identitasnya.

Sapuka Island
My classroom
My father
Sabuk Nusantara 52

General Topic

Topiknya umum, membahas kelompok atau jenis.

Islands
Classrooms
Fishermen
Boats

Text Structure

1. General Classification

Memperkenalkan topik secara umum. Jawab: What is it? dan What group does it belong to?

Seaweed is a marine plant that grows in the sea.

2. Description

Menjelaskan parts, features, habitat, function, behavior, importance, dan benefits.

It has many branches. It needs sunlight and clean seawater. It can be used for food.

7 • Grammar Lab

Language Features for Beginners

Belajar pelan-pelan: satu pola, banyak contoh, lalu gabungkan menjadi paragraf.

Simple Present Tense for Facts

Report Text memakai Simple Present karena membahas fakta umum, bukan kejadian kemarin.

Seaweed grows in the sea.
Coral reefs protect coastlines.
Fishermen catch fish for a living.
Boats carry people and goods.
❌ Seaweed grew in the sea.
✅ Seaweed grows in the sea.

General Nouns

Gunakan kata benda umum, bukan nama khusus.

SpecificGeneral
Sapuka Islandislands
My boatboats
My fatherfishermen
SMA Negeri 6 Pangkajene dan Kepulauanschools
Sabuk Nusantara 52passenger ships / boats

Linking Verbs: is / are

Use is for one thing or uncountable/general singular noun. Use are for plural nouns.

  • Seaweed is a marine plant.
  • A boat is a vehicle on water.
  • Coral reefs are underwater ecosystems.
  • Fishermen are people who catch fish.
  • Islands are land areas surrounded by water.
❌ Seaweed are a plant.
✅ Seaweed is a plant.

Has / Have

Use has for singular subjects and have for plural subjects.

  • Seaweed has many branches.
  • A boat has an engine.
  • Coral reefs have many sea animals.
  • Boats have engines and anchors.
  • Fishermen have nets, hooks, and fishing lines.

Can for Function

Use can + Verb 1 to explain function, ability, or use.

  • Coral reefs can reduce wave energy.
  • Boats can carry people and goods.
  • Starlink can help people access the internet.
  • Seaweed can be used for food and medicine.
  • Schools can support students’ future.

Sentence Patterns

A/An ... is a ...

A fisherman is a person who catches fish.

... are ...

Coral reefs are underwater ecosystems.

... live/grow in ...

Seaweed grows in seawater.

... are important because ...

Boats are important because they connect islands.

Build a Paragraph

Seaweed is a marine plant. It grows in seawater. It has many branches. It needs sunlight and clean water. Seaweed can be used for food and industry. It is important for coastal communities.

8 • Vocabulary Bank

50+ Words with Meanings and Local Examples

Bilingual Glossary
Classification Words 10 words
EnglishIndonesiaSimple ExampleLocal Context
animalhewanA fish is an animal.Many sea animals live near coral reefs.
planttumbuhanSeaweed is a plant.Seaweed grows in the sea around islands.
placetempatA school is a place for learning.A harbor is an important place for island travel.
objectbendaA net is an object used by fishermen.A rope is important in seaweed farming.
jobpekerjaanA fisherman is a job.Fishing is a common job in coastal communities.
toolalatA net is a fishing tool.Fishermen use tools such as nets and hooks.
vehiclekendaraanA boat is a vehicle.Boats are vehicles for traveling between islands.
ecosystemekosistemA coral reef is an ecosystem.Coral reefs support many sea animals.
communitymasyarakatA community is a group of people.Coastal communities depend on the sea.
resourcesumber dayaWater is a resource.Clean water is an important resource on small islands.
Nature and Sea 12 words
EnglishIndonesiaSimple ExampleLocal Context
seaweedrumput lautSeaweed grows in the sea.Seaweed is important for many island families.
coral reefterumbu karangA coral reef is an underwater ecosystem.Coral reefs can reduce wave energy near islands.
fishikanFish live in water.Fishermen catch fish for food and income.
lobsterlobsterLobsters live in the sea.Some coastal areas have lobsters.
turtlepenyu/kura-kura lautA turtle is a sea animal.Sea turtles need clean beaches and sea areas.
shellcangkang/kerangA shell protects some sea animals.Shells can be found near beaches.
sandpasirSand covers many beaches.White sand can be found on some small islands.
waveombakWaves move on the sea surface.Strong waves can affect boat travel.
tidepasang surutTides change sea levels.High tides can affect coastal villages.
islandpulauAn island is surrounded by water.Small islands need good transportation access.
coastpesisirThe coast is near the sea.Coastal areas are important for fishermen.
beachpantaiA beach is a sandy area near the sea.Beaches can be affected by erosion.
Features and Parts 11 words
EnglishIndonesiaSimple ExampleLocal Context
bodytubuh/badanA fish has a body and fins.Fish bodies have different shapes.
branchcabangSeaweed has branches.Healthy seaweed has many branches.
rootakarPlants usually have roots.Seaweed does not have roots like land plants.
leafdaunLeaves help plants make food.Coconut trees have long leaves.
enginemesinA boat has an engine.Boat engines help people travel farther.
deckdek kapalA deck is part of a boat.People can stand on the boat deck.
ropetaliA rope is used to tie things.Seaweed seedlings are tied to ropes.
netjaringA net is used to catch fish.Fishermen use nets in the sea.
classroomruang kelasA classroom is a room for learning.A school has classrooms for students.
roofatapA roof protects a building.Houses and schools have roofs.
walldindingA wall is part of a building.Classrooms have walls, doors, and windows.
Habitat and Place 8 words
EnglishIndonesiaSimple ExampleLocal Context
sealautThe sea is a large body of salt water.Many people on islands depend on the sea.
oceansamudraOceans cover large parts of Earth.Ocean waves can be very strong.
shallow waterperairan dangkalSome coral reefs grow in shallow water.Seaweed farms are usually placed in suitable shallow water.
deep waterperairan dalamSome fish live in deep water.Large boats may travel through deeper water.
villagedesa/kampungA village is a small community.Coastal villages are close to the sea.
coastal areawilayah pesisirA coastal area is near the sea.Coastal areas can experience high tides.
schoolsekolahA school is a place for learning.Schools in remote islands need learning resources.
harborpelabuhan/dermagaA harbor is a place for boats.Boats arrive and leave from a harbor.
Function and Use 10 words
EnglishIndonesiaSimple ExampleLocal Context
protectmelindungiCoral reefs protect coastlines.Coral reefs can reduce wave impact.
carrymembawaBoats carry people and goods.Boats carry students, teachers, and supplies.
connectmenghubungkanInternet connects people.Boats connect small islands with bigger towns.
supportmendukungSchools support education.Internet access supports online learning.
producemenghasilkanPlants produce food.Seaweed farming can produce useful materials.
providemenyediakanCoral reefs provide habitats.Schools provide learning spaces.
helpmembantuBoats help people travel.Starlink can help remote learning.
reducemengurangiCoral reefs reduce wave energy.Trees and reefs can reduce environmental impacts.
absorbmenyerapSome materials absorb water.Dry seaweed can absorb water again when it rains.
growtumbuhSeaweed grows in seawater.Seaweed grows better in suitable conditions.
Report Text Connectors 9 words
EnglishIndonesiaSimple ExampleLocal Context
alsojugaSeaweed is useful. It also supports coastal economies.Seaweed supports families. It also supports local work.
usuallybiasanyaFishermen usually work near the sea.Boats usually leave when the weather is safe.
generallyumumnyaCoral reefs generally grow in warm shallow seas.Small islands generally need boats for transport.
mostlysebagian besarSmall islands are mostly surrounded by seawater.Coastal people mostly depend on natural resources.
oftenseringBoats often carry people and goods.Students often use local examples to learn.
becausekarenaBoats are important because they connect islands.Clean water is important because people need it daily.
for examplemisalnyaFishermen use tools, for example nets and hooks.Small islands have resources, for example fish and seaweed.
in additionselain ituIn addition, coral reefs support sea life.In addition, schools build confidence.
howevernamunHowever, small islands can have limited access to resources.However, weather can affect transportation.

9 • Listening Zone

Listen, Predict, Complete, Discuss

TTS buttons included

Guru dapat membaca teks, siswa dapat membacakan berpasangan, atau gunakan tombol suara jika browser mendukung text-to-speech.

1. Seaweed

Seaweed is a marine plant. It grows in seawater. It can be green, brown, or red. Seaweed needs sunlight, clean water, and nutrients. Many coastal communities grow seaweed because it can be used for food and industry.

Pre-Listening

  1. Where does seaweed grow?
  2. What color is seaweed usually?
  3. Why do people grow seaweed?

While-Listening

  1. Seaweed is a __________.
  2. It grows in __________.
  3. It needs __________, clean water, and nutrients.

Post-Listening

  1. What is seaweed?
  2. Where does it grow?
  3. What does it need?
  4. Why do some communities grow seaweed?

2. Coral Reefs

Coral reefs are underwater ecosystems. They are made by small marine animals called coral polyps. Coral reefs usually grow in warm and shallow seawater. They provide homes for many kinds of fish, shells, and other sea animals. Coral reefs are also important because they can reduce wave energy before waves reach the shore.

Pre-Listening

  1. Have you ever seen coral reefs?
  2. What animals live near coral reefs?
  3. Why are coral reefs important?

While-Listening

  1. Coral reefs are underwater ecosystems.
  2. Coral reefs grow in warm and shallow seawater.
  3. Coral reefs provide homes for sea animals.

Post-Listening

  1. What are coral reefs?
  2. What are they made by?
  3. Where do they usually grow?
  4. Why are coral reefs important for shorelines?

3. Boats

Boats are vehicles used to travel on water. They can be small or large. Some boats use paddles, and some boats use engines. A boat usually has a body, a deck, ropes, an anchor, and sometimes a cabin. In island communities, boats are very important. They can carry people, food, school supplies, fuel, and other goods. Boats also help fishermen go to fishing areas and return home safely. For people who live on small islands, boats connect one place to another.

Pre-Listening

  1. What are boats used for?
  2. Why are boats important for island people?
  3. What parts of a boat do you know?

While-Listening

  1. Classification: Boats are __________.
  2. Parts: body, deck, ropes, anchor, __________.
  3. Function: Boats can carry __________ and goods.

Post-Listening

  1. What are boats?
  2. What are some parts of a boat?
  3. What can boats carry?
  4. How do boats help fishermen?

4. Small Islands Dialogue

Alya: What are small islands? Rafi: Small islands are land areas surrounded by water. Alya: Do small islands always have many people? Rafi: No. Some small islands have many people, but some islands have only a few people or no people. Alya: What can we find on small islands? Rafi: We can find beaches, coastal villages, trees, boats, and marine resources. Alya: Are small islands important? Rafi: Yes. They are important because they support communities, culture, fishing, and marine life. Alya: Do small islands have challenges? Rafi: Yes. Some small islands have limited clean water, limited transportation, and strong waves during certain seasons.

Pre-Listening

  1. What is a small island?
  2. What do small islands usually have?
  3. What challenges can small islands have?

While-Listening

  1. Small islands are surrounded by water.
  2. All small islands have many people.
  3. Small islands can have transportation challenges.

Post-Listening

  1. What are small islands?
  2. What can we find on small islands?
  3. Why are small islands important?
  4. What challenges can small islands have?

10 • Speaking Zone

Fact Talk and Mini Field Report

Fact Talk

A: What is seaweed?
B: Seaweed is a marine plant.
A: Where does it grow?
B: It grows in seawater.
A: Why is it important?
B: It is important because it can be used for food and industry.

Classify and Describe

Choose one topic and say: category + three facts.

Topic: Boats
Classification: Boats are vehicles used on water.
Fact 1: Boats can carry people.
Fact 2: Boats have engines, ropes, and anchors.
Fact 3: Boats are important for island transportation.

Dialogue Model: What Do You Know About Seaweed?

Nia: What do you know about seaweed?
Arman: Seaweed is a marine plant. It grows in seawater.
Nia: Does seaweed have flowers?
Arman: No, it does not have flowers like land plants. It has branches.
Nia: What does seaweed need to grow?
Arman: It needs sunlight, clean seawater, and nutrients.
Nia: Why is seaweed important?
Arman: It is important because people can use it for food and other products. It also supports coastal communities.

Mini Field Report Structure

1. What is it?2. What does it have?3. Where does it exist?4. What can it do?5. Why is it important?

11 • Reading Zone

8 Model Report Texts

Setiap teks dilengkapi visual, glossary, comprehension questions, fact-finding task, dan language analysis.

Short Report Text

1. Seaweed

General Classification

Seaweed is a marine plant. It grows in seawater and can be found in many coastal areas.

Description

Seaweed can be green, brown, or red. It has many branches and absorbs nutrients from the water. Seaweed needs sunlight, clean water, and good water movement. People can use seaweed for food, medicine, and industry. In many coastal communities, seaweed is also important as a source of income.

Glossary

marine plant = tumbuhan lautcoastal area = wilayah pesisirbranch = cabangnutrients = zat makananincome = penghasilan
Comprehension Questions
  1. What is seaweed?
  2. Where does seaweed grow?
  3. What colors can seaweed have?
  4. What does seaweed need?
  5. Why is seaweed important?
Fact-Finding Task

Find one fact about:

  • classification
  • color
  • needs
  • use
  • importance
Structure & Language Analysis
  • Simple Present: Seaweed is..., It grows..., It has..., It needs...
  • General noun: seaweed
  • Technical words: marine plant, nutrients, coastal area

Mini reflection: How is this topic important in island life?

Short Report Text

2. Boats

General Classification

Boats are vehicles used to travel on water. They are important in many coastal and island communities.

Description

Boats can be small or large. Some boats use engines, while others use paddles or sails. A boat usually has a body, a deck, ropes, and an anchor. Boats can carry people, goods, food, and fishing tools. For people on small islands, boats connect villages, schools, markets, and cities.

Glossary

vehicle = kendaraanpaddle = dayungsail = layardeck = dek kapalanchor = jangkargoods = barang
Comprehension Questions
  1. What are boats?
  2. Where are boats used?
  3. What are some parts of a boat?
  4. What can boats carry?
  5. Why are boats important for island people?
Fact-Finding Task

Find one fact about:

  • classification
  • parts
  • function
  • importance
Structure & Language Analysis
  • Boats are...
  • They are...
  • Boats can...
  • A boat usually has...

Mini reflection: How is this topic important in island life?

Medium Report Text

3. Coral Reefs

General Classification

Coral reefs are underwater ecosystems. They are made by tiny marine animals called coral polyps. Coral reefs usually grow in warm, clear, and shallow seawater.

Description

Coral reefs have many shapes and colors. They provide habitats for fish, shells, crabs, and many other sea animals. A healthy coral reef can support marine life and help keep the sea environment balanced.

Coral reefs are also important for coastal areas. They can reduce wave energy before waves reach the shore. Because of this, coral reefs can help protect beaches and small islands from strong waves and erosion.

Glossary

underwater = di bawah airecosystem = ekosistemcoral polyps = polip karanghabitat = tempat hidupmarine life = kehidupan lauterosion = abrasi/pengikisan
Comprehension Questions
  1. What are coral reefs?
  2. What makes coral reefs?
  3. Where do coral reefs usually grow?
  4. What animals can live around coral reefs?
  5. How can coral reefs protect coastal areas?
Fact-Finding Task

Find one fact about:

  • classification
  • habitat
  • animals
  • function
  • importance
Structure & Language Analysis
  • General nouns: coral reefs, ecosystems, animals, beaches, islands
  • Simple Present: are, grow, have, provide, support, reduce
  • Can for function: can reduce, can help protect

Mini reflection: How is this topic important in island life?

Medium Report Text

4. Small Islands

General Classification

Small islands are land areas surrounded by water. They are smaller than continents and are often located in seas, oceans, lakes, or rivers.

Description

Small islands can have beaches, rocks, trees, villages, and coastal areas. Some small islands have many people, while others have only a few people or no people. People who live on small islands often depend on the sea for food, transportation, and work.

Small islands can be beautiful and rich in natural resources. However, they can also have challenges. Some small islands have limited clean water, limited transportation, and strong waves during certain seasons. Because of this, island communities need good planning, cooperation, and environmental care.

Glossary

surrounded = dikelilingicontinent = benuadepend on = bergantung padanatural resources = sumber daya alamlimited = terbatascooperation = kerja sama
Comprehension Questions
  1. What are small islands?
  2. What can small islands have?
  3. What do island people often depend on?
  4. What challenges can small islands have?
  5. What do island communities need?
Fact-Finding Task

Find one fact about:

  • definition
  • features
  • people
  • resources
  • challenges
Structure & Language Analysis
  • Simple Present: are, have, depend, need
  • General nouns: small islands, people, communities
  • Connectors: however, because of this

Mini reflection: How is this topic important in island life?

Medium Report Text

5. Fishermen

General Classification

Fishermen are people who catch fish and other sea animals for a living. They usually work near rivers, lakes, seas, or oceans.

Description

Fishermen use many tools, such as boats, nets, hooks, ropes, and fishing lines. Some fishermen work alone, while others work in groups. Their work can start early in the morning, in the afternoon, or at night, depending on the weather, tide, and fishing method.

Fishermen are important in coastal communities because they provide fish for food and markets. Their work also supports families and local economies. However, fishing can be difficult and risky because fishermen face waves, strong winds, bad weather, and long hours at sea.

Glossary

for a living = sebagai mata pencaharianhook = kailfishing line = tali pancingmethod = cara/metodelocal economy = ekonomi lokalrisky = berisiko
Comprehension Questions
  1. What are fishermen?
  2. Where do fishermen usually work?
  3. What tools do fishermen use?
  4. Why are fishermen important?
  5. What challenges do fishermen face?
Fact-Finding Task

Find one fact about:

  • job
  • tools
  • time
  • importance
  • challenges
Structure & Language Analysis
  • Simple Present: are, catch, work, use, provide, support, face
  • General noun: fishermen
  • Connector: however

Mini reflection: How is this topic important in island life?

Longer Report Text

6. Starlink Internet

General Classification

Starlink is a satellite-based internet service. It uses satellites in space and a receiver on the ground to connect users to the internet. This kind of internet service can be useful in remote places where cable networks or cellular signals are limited.

Description

Starlink works by sending internet signals between ground stations, satellites, and user receivers. A receiver, often shaped like a flat dish, catches the signal from satellites. Then, the signal is shared with devices such as phones, laptops, tablets, or classroom screens through a router.

In remote island areas, internet access can support education, communication, and information sharing. Students can use the internet to access learning materials, teachers can find teaching resources, and families can communicate with people in other places. Internet access can also help communities receive news and important information.

However, satellite internet also needs electricity, proper equipment, and clear installation. Weather and technical problems may affect the connection. Because of this, users need to understand how to use and maintain the equipment responsibly.

Glossary

satellite-based = berbasis satelitreceiver = penerima sinyalremote places = tempat terpencilcellular signal = sinyal selulerrouter = alat pembagi koneksiequipment = peralatanmaintain = merawat
Comprehension Questions
  1. What is Starlink?
  2. What does it use to connect users to the internet?
  3. Why can it be useful in remote places?
  4. What devices can use the internet signal?
  5. What are some challenges of using satellite internet?
Fact-Finding Task

Find one fact about:

  • classification
  • parts/system
  • function
  • benefits
  • challenges
Structure & Language Analysis
  • Simple Present: is, uses, works, catches, supports, needs
  • Can for function: can be useful, can support, can use, can help
  • Technical vocabulary: satellite, receiver, router, signal, equipment

Mini reflection: How is this topic important in island life?

Longer Report Text

7. Schools in Remote Islands

General Classification

Schools in remote islands are educational institutions located far from large cities or main islands. They provide learning spaces for students who live in island communities.

Description

Schools in remote islands usually have classrooms, teachers, students, learning materials, and school facilities. Some schools may have limited facilities because of distance, transportation challenges, electricity problems, or internet access. However, these schools still play an important role in helping students learn and prepare for the future.

Teachers in remote island schools often need creativity and flexibility. They may use printed materials, local examples, group activities, and offline learning resources. Local context is very important because students can understand lessons better when the examples are close to their daily life.

Schools in remote islands are also important for communities. They support literacy, character building, communication skills, and future opportunities. A school is not only a place for lessons; it is also a place where students build confidence, discipline, cooperation, and dreams.

Glossary

remote = terpencileducational institution = lembaga pendidikanfacility = fasilitasflexibility = keluwesanprinted materials = bahan cetakliteracy = literasiopportunity = kesempatan
Comprehension Questions
  1. What are schools in remote islands?
  2. What do they usually have?
  3. What challenges can they face?
  4. Why do teachers need creativity?
  5. Why are these schools important for communities?
Fact-Finding Task

Find one fact about:

  • classification
  • facilities
  • challenges
  • teacher strategies
  • importance
Structure & Language Analysis
  • General nouns: schools, teachers, students, communities
  • Simple Present: are, provide, have, play, support
  • Can: can understand

Mini reflection: How is this topic important in island life?

Longer Report Text

8. Clean Water Resources on Small Islands

General Classification

Clean water resources are sources of water that people can use safely for daily needs. On small islands, clean water can come from rainwater, wells, water containers, or water transported from other places.

Description

Clean water is important for drinking, cooking, washing, cleaning, and health. People need clean water every day. On small islands, clean water can be limited because the land area is small, freshwater sources are few, and seawater surrounds the island.

During the rainy season, people can collect rainwater in tanks, buckets, jerry cans, or other containers. During the dry season, water can become more difficult to find. Some wells may become salty when seawater affects the groundwater. Because of this, people need to save water and use it carefully.

Clean water resources are important for families, schools, and communities. Without enough clean water, daily activities become difficult. Therefore, water conservation, rainwater storage, and community cooperation are important in small island life.

Glossary

resource = sumber dayasafely = dengan amandaily needs = kebutuhan sehari-haritransported = diangkutlimited = terbatasgroundwater = air tanahconservation = penghematan/pelestarianstorage = penyimpanan
Comprehension Questions
  1. What are clean water resources?
  2. Where can clean water come from on small islands?
  3. Why is clean water important?
  4. Why can clean water be limited on small islands?
  5. What can people do to save water?
Fact-Finding Task

Find one fact about:

  • classification
  • sources
  • uses
  • challenge
  • solution
Structure & Language Analysis
  • Simple Present: are, can come, is, need, collect, become
  • Can: can use, can come, can be limited
  • Connectors: because, during, therefore

Mini reflection: How is this topic important in island life?

12 • Viewing Zone

Read Visuals Like a Young Researcher

Classification chartGambar ini sudah tersedia di bagian Visual Assets. Gunakan visual unik di atas sebagai rujukan, tanpa memuat gambar yang sama dua kali.

Classification Chart

Use the chart to separate living and non-living things around Sapuka. This helps students organize information before writing factual reports.

  1. Which items are living things?
  2. Which items are non-living things?
  3. Why is classification useful for Report Text?
Small island map visualGambar ini sudah tersedia di bagian Visual Assets. Gunakan visual unik di atas sebagai rujukan, tanpa memuat gambar yang sama dua kali.

Map-Style Poster

Read the visual and complete factual sentences.

  1. Small islands are surrounded by __________.
  2. A small island can have beaches, villages, and __________.
  3. Boats help people travel between __________.
  4. Clean water is an important __________.

Seaweed Fact File

What is seaweed? Where does it grow? What does it need? What can people use it for?

Parts of a Boat

Label body, deck, engine, anchor, rope, cabin, and propeller.

Coral Reef Infographic

Find what coral reefs are, where they grow, and why they matter.

13 • Annotated Model Text

Seaweed

General Classification Seaweed is a marine plant that grows in seawater. It belongs to a group of simple plants that live in the sea.

Physical Features Seaweed can be green, brown, or red. It has many branches, but it does not have flowers like many land plants.

Habitat Seaweed grows in coastal waters. It needs sunlight, clean seawater, nutrients, and suitable water movement.

Function / Use Seaweed can be used for food, medicine, cosmetics, and industry. In some coastal communities, people grow seaweed as an important source of income.

Importance Seaweed is important because it supports people’s lives and coastal economies. It is also part of the marine environment.

14 • Common Mistakes Clinic

Wrong, Better, Why?

❌ Wrong

My seaweed farm is near my house.

✅ Better

Seaweed farms are places where people grow seaweed in the sea.

💡 Why?

Report Text explains general facts, not one personal object.

❌ Wrong

Seaweed grew in seawater.

✅ Better

Seaweed grows in seawater.

💡 Why?

Report Text uses Simple Present for general facts.

❌ Wrong

Coral reefs is important.

✅ Better

Coral reefs are important.

💡 Why?

Coral reefs is plural, so use are.

❌ Wrong

Boats has engines.

✅ Better

Boats have engines.

💡 Why?

Boats is plural, so use have.

❌ Wrong

Seaweed are useful plants.

✅ Better

Seaweed is a useful marine plant.

💡 Why?

Seaweed is commonly used as an uncountable/general noun.

15 • Guided Practice

From Recognition to Writing

A. Identify Specific or General

Write S or G: Sapuka Island, Islands, My boat, Boats, Fishermen, My father, Coral reefs, Schools, My school.

B. Match Topic and Classification

Seaweed → marine plant; Fishermen → people who catch fish; Boats → vehicles used on water; Coral reefs → underwater ecosystems; Schools → places for learning.

C. Choose is / are / has / have / can

  1. Seaweed ___ a marine plant.
  2. Boats ___ engines.
  3. Coral reefs ___ underwater ecosystems.
  4. Starlink ___ help remote communication.

D. Short Report Frame

__________ is/are __________. It/They live/grow/exist in __________. It/They have __________. It/They can __________. It/They are important because __________.

16 • Interactive Quiz

Check Your Understanding

15 Questions
Question 1 / 15
Score: 0

17 • Writing Mission

Write Your Own Factual Report

Beginner

6–8 sentences. Use the frame and focus on clear facts.

Intermediate

2 paragraphs, 100–150 words: classification + description.

Challenge

180–220 words with technical vocabulary and clear categories.

Topic Choices

SeaweedCoral ReefsSmall IslandsFishermenBoatsSchools in Remote IslandsStarlink InternetClean Water

Writing Template

Title:

Paragraph 1 — General Classification
What is it? What category does it belong to?

Paragraph 2 — Description
What does it look like? Where does it exist? What does it have? What can it do? Why is it important?

Sentence Starters

... is a ...... are ...... live/grow in ...... has/have ...... can ...... are important because ...

Draft Area

0 words

Self-Editing Checklist

18–19 • Presenting & Digital Creation

Share Your Report

Presentation Script

Good morning. Today, I will report about __________.
__________ is/are __________.
It belongs to __________.
It lives/grows/is found in __________.
It has __________.
It can __________.
It is important because __________.
Thank you.

Digital Creation Choices

  • fact card
  • infographic
  • mini poster
  • classification chart
  • field report page
  • carousel post
  • voice-over report

Rubric

CriteriaExcellentGoodNeeds Improvement
Factual AccuracyFacts are clear and correct.Most facts are correct.Some facts are unclear.
ClassificationTopic is introduced clearly.Topic is introduced simply.Classification is missing.
OrganizationIdeas are very well organized.Ideas are mostly organized.Ideas are confusing.
VocabularyUses suitable technical words.Uses some suitable words.Vocabulary is very limited.
Visual SupportVisual is clear and helpful.Visual is simple but useful.Visual is missing or unclear.

20 • Teacher Notes

Teaching Plan and Strategies

Meeting 1

Introduce Report Text, social function, and specific vs general.

Meeting 2

Read model texts, analyze structure, and practice grammar patterns.

Meeting 3

Writing workshop: choose topic, plan, draft, peer review, revise.

Meeting 4

Presentation and digital creation: fact card, poster, infographic.

For Lower-Level Students

  • Use sentence frames.
  • Start with one paragraph.
  • Allow bilingual vocabulary support.
  • Use pictures and real objects.
  • Let students speak before writing.

Offline Class Strategy

  • Print model texts and visuals.
  • Use board diagrams.
  • Use local objects: rope, net, shell, seaweed, bottle, water container.
  • Ask students to observe surroundings.

21 • Final Reflection

Observe Carefully. Report Confidently.

  1. What is Report Text?
  2. How is Report Text different from Descriptive Text?
  3. Why does Report Text use Simple Present Tense?
  4. What is general classification?
  5. What topic did you choose for your factual report?
  6. What new vocabulary did you learn?
  7. How can Report Text help you explain facts about your island, school, or community?

Final Message: Report Text helps us look at the world like young researchers. We observe, classify, collect facts, organize information, and share knowledge clearly.

Chapter 7 — Report Text • Factual Reports • Offline-first interactive material
